pi_items.verified_value: Portland cement (cement clinker production and ready-mix concrete materials available)
pi_items.source: USLCI FY24 Q4 database downloaded from https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content, confirmed via direct inspection of processes directory

Portland cement process (USLCI) contains 8 input flows with defaultProvider suppliers: Diesel combustion, Lubricant feedstock, Bituminous coal combustion, Residual fuel oil combustion, LPG combustion, Natural gas combustion, Electricity mix, Gasoline combustion
Task definition consistent with setup_task.sh which creates /home/ga/LCA_Results directory. File path follows standard openLCA export conventions for CSV results.

pi_items.verified_value: process_contribution analysis showing percentage contributions of upstream processes to total GWP
pi_items.source: README.md describes contribution analysis workflow: 'Process contribution tree showing upstream processes with their percentage contributions' is a standard openLCA 2.x feature accessible after LCIA calculation
Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 17-18 which creates and sets permissions for this directory
Script install_openlca.sh line 175 specifies USLCI FY24 Q4 URL. Download verified - 9.1MB valid zip file with processes, flows, actors, currencies, etc.
Script install_openlca.sh line 214-216 specifies Figshare ndownloader file ID. Download verified - 8.0MB valid zip containing TRACI 2.1 LCIA method with Global warming category

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 (built from LCIA Formatter v1.1.3 and Federal Elementary Flow List FEDEFL v1.2.3)
pi_items.source: LCIA methods package inspection confirms TRACI 2.1 method including 'Global warming' impact category essential for GWP calculation
TRACI 2.1 impact categories verified to include 'Global warming' along with Eutrophication, Acidification, Smog formation, Ozone depletion, and Human health categories

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available LCA data from authoritative sources: the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) database from NREL containing verified cement production processes, and TRACI 2.1 LCIA methods from the US EPA. Portland cement production in USLCI has 8 upstream process suppliers (exceeding the minimum of 4 required), including energy sources (natural gas, coal, diesel, electricity) and material inputs (lubricant, fuel oil, gasoline). The LCIA methods include Global Warming Potential calculations. All file paths and directories specified in metadata are correctly configured by the task setup scripts.
